title: Tree Traversal and Search
summary: Implement binary tree traversal and search operations, then explain traversal order and recursive structure.
studentInstructions: Create tree_algorithms.py containing a TreeNode class and traversal functions.
Implement preorder(root), inorder(root), postorder(root), breadth_first(root), and contains(root, target).
Each traversal function must return a list of visited values in order.
Create tests.py that builds at least three different trees and verifies traversal and search results.
Create traversal_note.md explaining the difference between the traversal orders and when each could be useful.

rubric.description: Preorder, inorder, postorder, and breadth-first traversal functions return correct visit orders for varied tree shapes.
The contains function correctly finds present values and rejects missing values, including for empty trees.
Recursive traversal functions use clear base cases and correctly combine results from subtrees.
Tests cover balanced, unbalanced, empty, and single-node trees where applicable.
The note accurately explains traversal order differences and gives plausible use cases for at least two traversal types.

gradingInstructions: Use only this assignment and its rubric.
Check required files before grading quality.
